system_prompt: You are an OpenFOAM debugging planner. Given current foam files, error logs and reviewer analysis, create a minimal rewrite plan. Output MUST be strict JSON only, with this exact schema: {"target_files": [{"file": "relative/path", "changes": "change1; change2"}]}. Rules: 1) Do not use markdown, backticks, or comments. 2) Use double quotes for all strings. 3) In changes, use short plain text actions separated by semicolons. 4) Do not include parentheses, backticks, or quote characters inside changes text. 5) Do not include run steps; only file edits.
user_prompt:
<foamfiles>list_foamfile=[FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "system";
    object      controlDict;
}

application     rhoCentralFoam;

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         0.2;

deltaT          0.0001;

writeControl    adjustableRunTime;

writeInterval   0.02;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  6;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable yes;

adjustTimeStep  no;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSchemes;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

fluxScheme      Kurganov;

ddtSchemes
{
    default         Euler;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(tauMC)      Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
    reconstruct(rho) vanLeer;
    reconstruct(U)  vanLeerV;
    reconstruct(T)  vanLeer;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    "(rho|rhoU|rhoE)"
    {
        solver          diagonal;
    }

    U
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        GaussSeidel;
        nSweeps         2;
        tolerance       1e-09;
        relTol          0.01;
    }

    e
    {
        $U;
        tolerance       1e-09;
        relTol          0.01;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='momentumTransport', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "constant";
    object      momentumTransport;
}

simulationType  laminar;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='physicalProperties', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "constant";
    object      physicalProperties;
}

thermoType
{
    type            hePsiThermo;
    mixture         pureMixture;
    transport       const;
    thermo          hConst;
    equationOfState perfectGas;
    specie          specie;
    energy          sensibleInternalEnergy;
}

mixture
{
    specie
    {
        molWeight   11640.3;
    }
    thermodynamics
    {
        Cp          2.5;
        Hf          0;
    }
    transport
    {
        mu          0;
        Pr          1.0;
    }
}'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='U', folder_name='0', content='FoamFile
{

// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

internalField   uniform (5 0 0);

boundaryField
{
    inlet
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform (5 0 0);
    }

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='T', folder_name='0', content='FoamFile

boundaryField
{
    inlet
    {

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='p', folder_name='0', content='FoamFile

dimensions      [1 -1 -2 0 0 0 0];

internalField   uniform 1;

boundaryField
{
    inlet

        type            fixedValue;

    {

        psi             thermo:psi;
        gamma           1.4;
        fieldInf        1;
        lInf            1;
        value           uniform 1;
    }

    {
        type            slip;
    }

    frontAndBack
    {
        type            empty;
    }
}

vertices

boundary

<review_analysis># Error Analysis and Resolution

## Error 1: `log.blockMesh` — Ill-defined primitive entry

**Error Message:**
```
"ill defined primitiveEntry starting at keyword 'vertices' on line 13 and ending at line 22"
```

This error has persisted across all 9 attempts. Every variation of the `vertices` format — single-line, multiline with/without commas, with/without comments — has failed. The parser is rejecting the entire `vertices` sub-dictionary.

## Error 2: `log.rhoCentralFoam` — Cannot find mesh

This is a cascading error from the failed `blockMesh`. Once `blockMesh` succeeds, this resolves automatically.

## Root Cause Analysis Across All Attempts

After 9 attempts with different vertex formats, the issue is not about commas, line breaks, or comments. The fundamental problem is that **this version of OpenFOAM (Foundation version) expects the `vertices` sub-dictionary to be formatted in a very specific way that differs from modern OpenFOAM versions.**

Looking at the error message pointing to line 13-22 of the `vertices` sub-dictionary, and examining the actual file content, I believe the issue is that the `vertices` dictionary is using the newer OpenFOAM-style format (with named entries and comments) but this Foundation version expects the **older, stricter format**.

## Proposed Solution — Legacy Format That Works

Based on historical OpenFOAM Foundation version behavior, the `vertices` sub-dictionary must be formatted as a **simple list of coordinate triples**, with **no additional syntax** — no comments, no inline labels, no special separators beyond whitespace and parentheses.

### `blockMeshDict` — `vertices` section

Replace the current `vertices` block with this **minimalist legacy format**:

```
vertices
(
    (-0.15242 -0.005 -0.005)
    (0.3048 -0.005 -0.005)
    (0.3048 0.1524 -0.005)
    (-0.15242 0.1524 -0.005)
    (-0.15242 -0.005 0.005)
    (0.3048 -0.005 0.005)
    (0.3048 0.1524 0.005)
    (-0.15242 0.1524 0.005)
);
```

Key features:
- Exactly 8 entries (one for each corner of the rectangular prism)
- Each entry on its own line
- **Only whitespace and parentheses** — no commas, no semicolons, no inline comments, no labels
- No blank lines between entries
- Parentheses closed immediately after the third coordinate

### `blocks` section

Keep the current single-block definition:

```
blocks
(
    hex (0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7) (30 30 2) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
);
```

### `boundary` section

Keep the current boundary patch definitions unchanged — they are all correct and match the user requirements.

## Why This Should Work

This is the format that early versions of OpenFOAM (Foundation series) expected for the `vertices` dictionary — a raw list of coordinate triples with no syntactic sugar. Modern versions accept both formats, but this particular Foundation version is strict and only accepts this minimalist style.

## Summary of Changes

1. **`blockMeshDict`**: Replace the `vertices` sub-dictionary with the 8-entry legacy format shown above — no commas, no inline comments, no extra syntax, just one coordinate triple per line.
2. **All other files** (`controlDict`, `fvSchemes`, `fvSolution`, `momentumTransport`, `physicalProperties`, initial/boundary fields): No changes needed — they already conform to the user requirements.

This approach bypasses all the persistent parsing issues by using the exact format this version of OpenFOAM expects from the `vertices` sub-dictionary.</review_analysis>
<user_requirement>Perform a 2D supersonic flow simulation over a wedge. Create a 3D geometry spanning from -0.15242 m to 0.3048 m in the x-direction, 0 m to 0.1524 m in the y-direction, and -0.005 m to 0.005 m in the z-direction. The simulation starts at 0.0 seconds and runs until 0.2 seconds with a time step (deltaT) of 0.0001 seconds. The results are written every 0.02 seconds. The inlet velocity is 5 m/s, inlet temperature is 1 K and inlet pressure is 1 Pa. The wedge boundary uses slip boundary condition for velocity. Use normalized thermodynamic properties where molWeight is set to 11640.3, Cp is 2.5, Pr is 1.0, and dynamic viscosity mu is 0. This corresponds to a gas constant R of 0.7143 and a specific heat ratio of 1.4. Use rhoCentralFoam solver.</user_requirement>
Return strict JSON now with key target_files only.
response: target_files=[PlannedFileChange(file='system/blockMeshDict', changes='Replace vertices sub-dictionary with legacy format: exactly 8 coordinate triples, one per line, enclosed in parentheses, no commas, no inline comments, no labels, no blank lines between entries')]
